build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors.

requirements.description: The player interacts with cooking stations by clicking to place ingredients and initiate cooking processes with visible timers or progress indicators. Score 0 if there are no interactive stations. Score 0.5 at most if stations exist but have no cooking process or timer. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Orders arrive with recipe requirements and countdown timers; failing to complete an order before timeout loses it with visible negative feedback. Score 0 if no order system exists. Score 0.5 at most if orders appear but have no time pressure or failure consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Recipes require multiple steps at different stations in a specific order (chop, cook, assemble, plate), and the player must manage the sequence. Score 0 if recipes are single-step. Score 0.5 at most if multiple steps exist but order does not matter. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Overcooking or burning food wastes ingredients with visible smoke/fire feedback, requiring the player to monitor multiple timers simultaneously. Score 0 if food cannot be overcooked. Score 0.5 at most if overcooking is possible but has no visual warning or consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Completing orders earns coins and tips based on speed, which fund upgrades between shifts. Score 0 if no economy exists. Score 0.5 at most if coins are earned but cannot be spent on anything. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 5 distinct cooking stations (chopping board, stove, fryer, oven, plating area) with different interaction mechanics. Score 0 if only one station exists. Score 0.5 at most if stations differ only in name with identical behavior. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 8 distinct recipes with different ingredient combinations and station sequences, unlocked progressively. Score 0 if only one recipe exists. Score 0.5 at most if recipes differ only in name with identical steps. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Between-shift upgrades offer station improvements (faster stove, larger fryer), new recipe unlocks, and kitchen expansions with at least 5 options. Score 0 if no upgrade system exists. Score 0.5 at most if upgrades are listed but produce no observable change. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 10 shifts with escalating difficulty: more simultaneous orders, complex recipes, shorter timers, and higher customer expectations. Score 0 if difficulty never increases. Score 0.5 at most if shifts increase only in order count with no new recipes or complexity. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A shift summary shows orders completed, orders failed, tips earned, star rating, and total coins with retry or continue options. Score 0 if shifts end abruptly with no summary. Score 0.5 at most if only coins earned are shown. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Normal startup shows a styled title screen with the game name, restaurant storefront imagery, and a Start control. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse text on a blank background. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The flow connects title to shift start to kitchen gameplay to shift summary to upgrade shop without restarting the executable. Score 0 if major transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if transitions exist but break visually. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The HUD clearly shows active orders with recipes and timers, current station states, and coins earned in readable non-overlapping regions. Score 0 if the player cannot determine what to cook or order status. Score 0.5 at most if information is present but overlapping or unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Player actions produce visible feedback: placing ingredients shows them on stations, cooking shows progress, burning shows smoke, completing orders shows success animation, and tips flash. Score 0 if actions happen silently. Score 0.5 at most if only one feedback type exists. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Art direction is coherent across title, kitchen, stations, food items, orders, HUD, and menus with a consistent restaurant palette and style. Score 0 if mostly default controls or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Cooking stations, ingredients, prepared dishes, kitchen equipment, and order tickets use real sprite art with appetizing distinct designs. Score 0 if primary elements are unstyled rectangles. Score 0.5 at most if art exists but is inconsistent in style. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation brings the kitchen to life: cooking bubbles, chopping motion, fire flickering, steam rising, plating assembly, and order-complete celebrations. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if only one element animates. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI panels use styled themed widgets with chalkboard menus, recipe cards, or restaurant-styled elements rather than raw Godot defaults. Score 0 if all UI is default Label/Button. Score 0.5 at most if some panels are styled but others remain raw defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
